themes.title: Far-right parties push 'national priority' policies
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right parties across Europe are intensifying their focus on 'national priority' policies, which prioritize citizens over immigrants in access to housing, social benefits, and public services. This theme is particularly prominent in Spain (Vox), Italy (Fratelli d'Italia/Lega), and France (Rassemblement National), with opposition parties launching coordinated counter-campaigns.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Demands 'national priority' in Andalusian elections, criticizing PP-PSOE for favoring immigrants over locals in housing and social services.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Supports 'national priority' in housing policies, with Salvini announcing bonuses for separated parents.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Marine Le Pen advocates for taxing TotalEnergies' profits to fund fuel price caps, framing it as a national priority issue.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Promotes 'British priority' in local elections, criticizing Labour for failing to address housing and immigration.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Criticizes opposition for undermining national priorities in NATO and EU alliances.

themes.title: Trump’s Iran war dominates global right-wing coverage
themes.blurb: Right-wing media in the U.S., UK, France, Italy, and Spain are intensely focused on Trump's handling of the Iran war, including his rejection of a new peace proposal, military strategies, and the broader geopolitical implications. This theme reflects a transnational alignment in framing the conflict as a defining issue of Trump's presidency.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Trump reviews and rejects Iran's peace proposal, escalating tensions and justifying military actions.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Covers Trump's Iran strategy as a key issue in UK local elections, framing it as a test of Western resolve.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Criticizes Macron for failing to address Iran's threat to European security, aligning with Trump's hardline stance.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni supports U.S. actions in Iran, positioning Italy as a key NATO ally in the conflict.

countries.name: 🇪🇸 Vox
countries.note: Frames Iran war as a clash of civilizations, criticizing PSOE for weak stance on national security.

themes.title: Meloni’s government longevity sparks cross-national debate
themes.blurb: Giorgia Meloni's government in Italy has become the second-longest in the country's post-war history, sparking debates in Italy, Germany, Hungary, and France about the implications of far-right governance, economic policies, and EU relations. This theme highlights the transnational fascination with Meloni's political durability and its broader significance.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Meloni's government reaches 1,288 days in power, becoming Italy's second-longest post-war government.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: Debates Meloni's economic policies as a model for AfD's vision of Germany's future.

countries.name: 🇭🇺 Fidesz / Tisza
countries.note: Compares Meloni's longevity to Orbán's rule, analyzing its impact on Hungary's political landscape.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Marine Le Pen praises Meloni's stability while criticizing Macron's handling of EU relations.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: Analyzes Meloni's government as a case study in far-right governance and its implications for Poland.

themes.title: Far-right surges in European polls and elections
themes.blurb: Far-right parties in Germany (AfD), France (Rassemblement National), Sweden (Sverigedemokraterna), Norway (Fremskrittspartiet), and the UK (Reform UK) are experiencing significant gains in polls and local elections. This theme reflects a broader transnational trend of far-right resurgence, driven by issues like immigration, economic dissatisfaction, and cultural identity.

countries.name: 🇩🇪 Alternative für Deutschland
countries.note: AfD leads polls in Saxony-Anhalt and Thuringia, gaining ground ahead of September elections.

countries.name: 🇫🇷 Rassemblement National
countries.note: Marine Le Pen and Jordan Bardella lead polls for the 2027 presidential election, capitalizing on economic and immigration concerns.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: SD gains in polls, with Jimmie Åkesson refusing to debate Magdalena Andersson ahead of elections.

countries.name: 🇳🇴 Fremskrittspartiet
countries.note: FrP leads polls in Norway, with Sylvi Listhaug positioning herself as a potential prime minister.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK targets Labour strongholds in local elections, gaining traction with anti-immigration and anti-establishment rhetoric.

themes.title: Far-right rhetoric fuels political violence
themes.blurb: Right-wing and far-right rhetoric in the U.S., UK, Italy, and Sweden is increasingly linked to political violence, including assassination attempts, protests, and threats. This theme highlights the transnational consequences of inflammatory language and its role in escalating tensions.

countries.name: 🇺🇸 MAGA / Trump
countries.note: Third assassination attempt on Trump sparks debates about heated political rhetoric and security failures.

countries.name: 🇬🇧 Reform UK
countries.note: Reform UK candidates face threats and assaults during local election campaigns, linked to anti-immigration rhetoric.

countries.name: 🇮🇹 Fratelli d'Italia / Lega
countries.note: Anarchists attack FdI election booths, sparking debates about political polarization and violence.

countries.name: 🇸🇪 Sverigedemokraterna
countries.note: Far-right marchers assault counter-protesters in Sweden, highlighting rising political tensions.

countries.name: 🇵🇱 Prawo i Sprawiedliwość
countries.note: PiS criticizes opposition for fueling political conflict, warning of institutional collapse.
